requirements.text: Add a section titled 'Owner-directive compliance', <=12 lines, to root CLAUDE.md.
requirements.required_evidence: CLAUDE.md contains the titled section and its body is <=12 lines.

requirements.text: Do NOT put the full workflow in CLAUDE.md and do NOT create a competing .claude/CLAUDE.md.
requirements.required_evidence: No .claude/CLAUDE.md exists; section is a pointer, not the protocol.

requirements.text: CLAUDE.md section defines a substantive owner directive (changes/corrects/restricts/authorizes/replans/implements/dispatches/claims/accepts/merges/amends) and excludes pure explanations and read-only status questions unless they introduce a new requirement.
requirements.required_evidence: Section text contains the definition and the exclusion clause.

requirements.text: CLAUDE.md section requires, before planning/writing/claiming/dispatching/submitting/accepting/merging/declaring-completion for an applicable directive: invoke /directive-compliance; capture under project-control/directives/; bind task/PR to atomic requirement IDs; preserve prohibitions/holds/sequencing/dependencies/harnesses/evidence/owner-decisions/return-items; require independent completeness+final verification; refuse narrative 'all addressed' as evidence.
requirements.required_evidence: Each of the six obligations appears in the section.

requirements.text: Keep the existing automatic-context (eager) budget green.
requirements.required_evidence: context_budget_check.py passes before and after; eager total <= 6000.

requirements.text: Create .claude/skills/directive-compliance/SKILL.md.
requirements.required_evidence: File exists with description-only frontmatter (pack convention).

requirements.text: Skill description auto-triggers on owner directives, corrections, PR amendments, replans, 'fix everything', 'address all items', implementation authorizations, and completion claims; remains manually invocable as /directive-compliance.
requirements.required_evidence: Description contains each trigger surface.

requirements.text: Skill workflow includes every listed step.
requirements.required_evidence: Each checklist step present in SKILL.md body.

checklist.text: classify whether the prompt is a substantive directive
capture the exact text verbatim before acting
create append-only amendment files instead of silently rewriting history
decompose every instruction into atomic requirements

separately capture positive deliverables, prohibitions, holds, sequencing, dependencies, decisions, unresolved questions, acceptance harnesses, evidence requirements, external-current-fact checks, and required return-report items
forward trace from every source paragraph/item to requirement IDs
reverse trace from every requirement and changed file back to its source
stop for genuine ambiguity instead of choosing silently
bind the applicable IDs to task packets and review packets
invalidate earlier verification when reviewed head SHA or directive source changes
prohibit 'complete'/'all addressed' while any item is pending/failed/blocked/unverifiable/stale/missing-evidence

requirements.text: Create a versioned structure under project-control/directives/.
requirements.required_evidence: Directory tree exists with schema/v1 and one dir per directive.

requirements.text: index.json registry supports MULTIPLE concurrent active directives (not a single global current-directive pointer).
requirements.required_evidence: Two active directives coexist without eviction.

requirements.text: Provide versioned schema files.
requirements.required_evidence: schema/v1/*.schema.json present.

requirements.text: One directory per directive.
requirements.required_evidence: D-001 has its own directory.

requirements.text: Verbatim source-001.md per directive.
requirements.required_evidence: source-001.md byte-verbatim; hash matches manifest.

requirements.text: Append-only source-00N-amendment.md files; never rewrite a committed source.
requirements.required_evidence: Amendment is a new file; rewriting source-001 without a new amendment fails the validator.

requirements.text: Per-directive manifest.json, requirements.json, verification.json present.
requirements.required_evidence: All three files present and schema-valid.

requirements.text: Do NOT use one global 'current directive' pointer that loses parallel work.
requirements.required_evidence: index.json is a keyed list; adding a 2nd active directive does not evict the 1st.

requirements.text: Registry maps each directive to exact tasks, branches, PRs, scopes, and statuses.
requirements.required_evidence: manifest fields affected_tasks/affected_prs/scope/status populated.

requirements.text: manifest.json includes at least: directive ID+version; capture timestamp; source-file hashes; frozen baseline SHA; affected tasks/PRs; scope+applicability; amendments+supersession; owner-approval state; lifecycle state; final reviewed SHA when applicable.
requirements.required_evidence: Validator asserts each key present.

requirements.text: Each atomic requirement includes at least: stable ID; exact source anchor; plain-language obligation; classification; affected scope+paths; dependencies+sequencing; required harness/test; required evidence; producer; independent verifier; status+reason; evidence paths; reviewed SHA; supersession/NOT_APPLICABLE justification where relevant.
requirements.required_evidence: Validator asserts each key present per requirement row.

requirements.text: Capture THIS setup directive as the first live directive (D-001) and prove the system against its own implementation.
requirements.required_evidence: validate_directive_compliance.py --check passes over D-001; M0-T023 cites D-001.

requirements.text: Do NOT create a competing lifecycle authority; extend tools/project_control.py and its atomic-write + orchestrator-authority model.
requirements.required_evidence: New checks add reasons to accept()'s existing list; no override/skip flag; no parallel state machine.

requirements.text: A new or materially-amended (in-regime) task cannot be CLAIMED without applicable directive references.
requirements.required_evidence: claim of an in-regime task without directive_refs is refused.

requirements.text: SUBMISSION cannot proceed without implementation evidence for every applicable requirement.
requirements.required_evidence: submit without an evidence map covering applicable requirements is refused.

requirements.text: ACCEPTANCE cannot proceed without independent final verification of every applicable requirement at the same frozen evidence identity.
requirements.required_evidence: accept of an in-regime task without a matching-identity final verification gate is refused.

requirements.text: A source amendment or head-identity change invalidates stale verification.
requirements.required_evidence: verify at identity A then change to B -> prior PASS is stale and does not satisfy accept.

requirements.text: Producer and verifier identities must differ.
requirements.required_evidence: Reuses existing gate/accept producer!=reviewer enforcement; a same-identity final verification is refused.

requirements.text: NOT_APPLICABLE requires explicit reasoning and independent approval.
requirements.required_evidence: A NOT_APPLICABLE state without justification+independent approver fails the validator.

requirements.text: BLOCKED/FAIL/UNVERIFIABLE/pending/stale/missing-evidence prevents acceptance.
requirements.required_evidence: Any such verification state blocks accept.

requirements.text: Do NOT retroactively rewrite or reject accepted historical tasks.
requirements.required_evidence: All 42 accepted packets remain byte-stable; terminal-state guards untouched.

requirements.text: Define an effective version/date and a safe machine-enforceable migration rule for existing nonaccepted work.
requirements.required_evidence: DIRECTIVE_REGIME_EFFECTIVE recorded; regime-entry transitions coded.

requirements.text: Prove migration does not deadlock currently claimed or awaiting-gate work.
requirements.required_evidence: Every current in-flight task transitions without directive_refs under the grandfather rule (migration table).

requirements.text: /start-controlled-task requires exact directive requirement references.
requirements.required_evidence: SKILL.md claim-gate names directive/requirement IDs.

requirements.text: /replan-project captures new owner information or corrections as a directive/amendment before changing the plan.
requirements.required_evidence: New capture-before-change step added.

requirements.text: /submit-checkpoint requires a requirement-to-evidence mapping.
requirements.required_evidence: Inclusion list requires the map; template section added.

requirements.text: /run-quality-gate requires directive verification at the frozen SHA and forbids relying on the producer's summary.
requirements.required_evidence: Skill adds per-directive re-derivation at frozen identity.

requirements.text: Update .claude/rules/project-control.md with concise path-scoped rules for the new directive records.
requirements.required_evidence: Directives-registry rules section added.

requirements.text: Amend these skills WITHOUT duplicating the full protocol.
requirements.required_evidence: Each amendment is a short reference clause, not a protocol copy.

requirements.text: Create .claude/agents/directive-compliance-verifier.md.
requirements.required_evidence: File exists with reviewer frontmatter.

requirements.text: Verifier is custom, read-only, governed by existing reviewer restrictions.
requirements.required_evidence: Name added to READ_ONLY_AGENTS frozenset AND to the guard test roster; denied mutating Bash.

requirements.text: Verifier must NOT treat the producer report or producer-created matrix as proof of completeness.
requirements.required_evidence: Agent body states matrix/report is a claim, never proof.

requirements.text: At intake the verifier compares the verbatim directive and every amendment against the atomic matrix and returns PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED with missing/weakened/combined/invented requirements.
requirements.required_evidence: Intake behavior specified in agent body.

requirements.text: At final review the verifier inspects the original directive+amendments, atomic requirements, frozen baseline+head SHAs, actual diff+files, tests/harness outputs, task+PR state, prohibited-action evidence, and required return items.
requirements.required_evidence: All inputs enumerated in agent body.

requirements.text: Verifier reports every requirement ID individually; 'spot checked', 'appears complete', and summary-only verification are prohibited.
requirements.required_evidence: Agent body forbids summary-only verification.

requirements.text: Create a stdlib-only read-only validator tools/validate_directive_compliance.py.
requirements.required_evidence: Validator imports only stdlib; runs read-only with --check.

requirements.text: Add comprehensive tests and wire them into the existing control-plane CI (connected, not an orphan green).
requirements.required_evidence: New steps added to ci.yml control-plane job.

requirements.text: Validator covers at least the listed checks.
requirements.required_evidence: Each check has a positive and negative test.

checklist.text: stale verification after source or head changes
no silent disappearance of requirements through supersession
no retroactive mutation of accepted packets
safe handling of multiple simultaneous directives

requirements.text: No new package may be installed; if any dependency is proposed, stop and apply /dependency-security.
requirements.required_evidence: No lockfile change; all new tools stdlib-only.

requirements.text: Create a small hook that reads ONLY the active directive registry and injects a compact reminder + active directive paths at session startup/resume, after compaction, and on UserPromptSubmit (short conditional reminder, not the entire protocol).
requirements.required_evidence: Hook registered on SessionStart+UserPromptSubmit; reads registry only; additionalContext + exit 0.

requirements.text: Per-prompt injected text remains small and says, in substance, to invoke /directive-compliance and capture/bind before acting when the prompt changes repository work.
requirements.required_evidence: Injected text under a fixed byte cap and contains the substance line.

requirements.text: Add narrowly scoped deterministic action enforcement only after adversarial tests pass; it must NOT block read-only inspection or test execution; it SHOULD block applicable producer dispatch or controlled lifecycle transitions when the task lacks valid directive references or the validator fails; any merge guard preserves explicit owner-approval requirements.
requirements.required_evidence: CLI lifecycle enforcement blocks in-regime transitions lacking refs; read/status/test commands pass through.

requirements.text: Do NOT replace or weaken agent_dispatch_guard.py, readonly_agent_guard.py, their tests, ADR-005, or existing authority rules.
requirements.required_evidence: Existing guard files change only additively (roster append); their existing tests still pass.

requirements.text: Include positive and negative tests proving at least the listed scenarios.
requirements.required_evidence: Each scenario has a named test.

checklist.text: two materially different requirements incorrectly combined
an amendment captured but not added to the matrix
source text changed without hash/version change
implementation verified at identity A then changed at B
two concurrent directives with different scopes
existing claimed task is not accidentally deadlocked
context compaction restores the short active pointer
hooks do not inject the full protocol on every prompt
governance files cannot be modified by ordinary product tasks without an applicable governance directive

requirements.text: Use this directive's own matrix during implementation.
requirements.required_evidence: requirements.json drives the return; the return is generated from it.

requirements.text: After freezing the PR head, use the new read-only directive-compliance-verifier plus the existing required control/security reviewers.
requirements.required_evidence: Independent reviews recorded at the frozen head identity.

requirements.text: Re-run all existing control-plane, hook, context-budget, and repository CI checks.
requirements.required_evidence: test_project_control.py, guard tests, context_budget_check.py all pass locally + in CI.

requirements.text: Return the full evidence list.
requirements.required_evidence: Return contains each listed item.

checklist.text: branch, PR, base SHA, final head SHA
every requirement ID with PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED/UNVERIFIABLE status and evidence
migration behavior for accepted and active work
local and GitHub CI results stated separately

requirements.text: Do NOT claim infallibility or guaranteed semantic completeness; state precisely what is mechanically enforced, independently reviewed, and what still requires owner/qualified-human judgment.
requirements.required_evidence: Return states the three tiers explicitly.

requirements.text: Stop after opening the PR and returning the evidence; do NOT merge.
requirements.required_evidence: No merge/accept performed; PR left open.

requirements.text: Requirement extraction before editing: freeze+report base SHA; copy directive verbatim into a durable matrix; assign stable IDs; do not combine unrelated requirements; do not omit qualifications/negatives/stop-conditions; return the requirement-to-file/test map before implementation; stop on ambiguity/contradiction.
requirements.required_evidence: Base SHA frozen; verbatim captured; matrix returned pre-implementation; R-000 contradiction surfaced.

requirements.text: One PR at a time; never describe proposed/unmerged changes as current-main state; state each branch's actual vs hypothetical combined ledger.
requirements.required_evidence: This work is one PR; current vs proposed state stated separately.

requirements.text: One current source of truth: archive superseded instructions; sweep the whole changed tree for stale IDs/counts/dates/branches/worktrees/ownership.
requirements.required_evidence: Stale-reference sweep run over the final diff.

requirements.text: Implemented only when the diff contains the change; PASS only when evidence executed/inspected; prove every exhaustive claim; generate the return from the matrix, not a free-form report.
requirements.required_evidence: Return generated from requirements.json; exhaustive claims backed by commands.

requirements.text: Semantic + stale-reference sweep before independent review; distinguish machine enforcement vs structural prevention vs manual procedure vs proposal-only; verify every claimed automatic behavior has executable code.
requirements.required_evidence: Enforcement-tier table produced; each automatic claim points to code.

requirements.text: External/time-sensitive facts protocol (query primary source at execution time; exact timestamps; record URL+retrieval+value; stop on data_conflict).
requirements.required_evidence: No external threshold fact required to build this stdlib-only governance system; documented as such.

requirements.text: Clean-context independent verification: a separate verifier that did not produce the changes; not given the producer's conclusion; marks every requirement PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED/UNVERIFIABLE; any FAIL/UNVERIFIABLE prevents completion.
requirements.required_evidence: directive-compliance-verifier runs read-only at frozen head; per-ID verdicts.

requirements.text: Add machine regressions for the listed check families (task/ledger counts; milestone summaries; task IDs+dependency direction; blocker records; prohibited old identifiers; contract closure; fixture-only work; cross-PR state; no missing matrix rows).
requirements.required_evidence: Validator + tests cover the listed families where applicable to this task.

requirements.text: Return standard + no-'complete' claim while any row is FAIL/UNVERIFIABLE or the verifier disagrees or the diff/branch was not reconciled after the last change.
requirements.required_evidence: Completion language gated on zero FAIL/UNVERIFIABLE + verifier agreement.

requirements.text: Owner boundary: passing this protocol authorizes nothing (no merge/task-movement/claim/dispatch/out-of-scope implementation/acceptance/deployment/purchasing/dependency-install/PR-closure).
requirements.required_evidence: No such action taken; existing approvals still required.

requirements.text: This directive authorizes creating/moving-to-ready/claiming/implementing EXACTLY ONE governance task (lead-only, dedicated branch, one PR) if the reconciled ledger and concurrency rules permit.
requirements.required_evidence: One task M0-T023 on one branch; concurrency verified (lead-only, non-overlapping scope).

requirements.text: This directive does NOT authorize accepting/merging this task; modifying/closing PRs #64/#91/#93/#94/#95; dispatching product work; changing product code; deploying; purchasing; installing dependencies; or weakening any blocker/hold/gate/owner-approval.
requirements.required_evidence: None of these actions performed (confirmed in return).

requirements.text: Reconcile origin/main, ledger, claimed/blocked tasks, worktrees, and open PRs; freeze+report the base SHA; create the new task ID only after reconciliation; do not reuse/alter an accepted task.
requirements.required_evidence: Reconciliation done; base 1acb9b5 frozen; M0-T023 is a new id.

requirements.text: project_control.py stays the only acceptance authority but its claim/submit/accept checks use a SHARED stdlib-only, read-only directive resolver to verify each cited directive/requirement exists, is active, has valid hashes, and applies; nonexistent/withdrawn/superseded/malformed/hash-invalid references fail closed; the standalone validator and the CLI share the same resolver.
requirements.required_evidence: tools/directive_registry.py imported by both CLI and validator; bad refs fail closed.

requirements.text: Define deterministic applicability metadata for each directive requirement (task IDs/types, milestones, relevant paths, lifecycle event, effective date).
requirements.required_evidence: Every requirement carries an applicability object.

requirements.text: Derive the applicable active requirements and compare against the task's directive_refs; a task cannot satisfy compliance by citing only the easiest applicable requirements (no cherry-picking; full coverage required).
requirements.required_evidence: A task citing a strict subset of applicable requirements is refused.

requirements.text: Unknown applicability, conflicting active requirements, or unresolved scope must BLOCK controlled work and require an existing blocker/owner decision (never silently choose).
requirements.required_evidence: Resolver returns unresolved -> CLI blocks the transition and points to blocker/owner decision.

requirements.text: Define explicit directive states: proposed, active, superseded, withdrawn.
requirements.required_evidence: index/manifest status uses the state vocabulary; resolver honors it.

requirements.text: AI may draft a directive but may not self-activate, amend, supersede, or withdraw an owner directive.
requirements.required_evidence: owner_approval + activation are owner-gated; documented in rules and manifest.

requirements.text: After activation, original verbatim source files, hashes, and requirement IDs become append-only; corrections use numbered amendments with supersession links; CI rejects silent deletion, renumbering, or rewriting of active source/requirements.
requirements.required_evidence: Rewriting a source without a new amendment, or dropping/renumbering an active requirement id, fails the validator.

requirements.text: Replace 'materially amended' with machine-enforceable regime-entry transitions (e.g. replan, rework/reclaim, specified packet-field changes); do not rely on original created_at alone; accepted/canceled tasks untouched.
requirements.required_evidence: A legacy nonterminal task entering rework/reclaim is stamped in-regime and then requires refs; created_at alone does not keep it grandfathered after rework.

requirements.text: Test every relevant current task state and prove no existing task is deadlocked (migration table).
requirements.required_evidence: A test enumerates each current status and shows a legal path forward.

requirements.text: Define frozen-evidence identity as a path-scoped content manifest (hash of relevant changed-file contents) plus the reviewed SHA as provenance; identical content survives merge/rebase/squash; any relevant product/control-file change after review invalidates the affected PASS evidence; acceptance never consumes evidence for different relevant contents.
requirements.required_evidence: Content-manifest match, not raw SHA equality, gates final verification.

requirements.text: Add adversarial tests for post-review edits, identical-tree merges, merge commits, rebases, and squash merges.
requirements.required_evidence: Each scenario asserts correct stale/fresh behavior.

requirements.text: The reminder hook injects only validated applicable requirement IDs, short imperative summaries, and file pointers; never raw directive source text.
requirements.required_evidence: Injected text contains IDs/summaries/pointers only; no verbatim source.

requirements.text: Strict size limit on reminder output; avoid repeating the same full reminder on every prompt or compaction (dedup).
requirements.required_evidence: Output <= cap; repeated identical prompt yields a shortened/deduped reminder.

requirements.text: An invalid/corrupt registry must produce a visible fail-closed warning; it must never silently emit nothing and continue.
requirements.required_evidence: A corrupt registry yields a visible warning (not empty output).

requirements.text: Treat registry text as inert data so it cannot become a prompt-injection or command-execution surface.
requirements.required_evidence: Registry content is never executed; only IDs/pointers/whitelisted summary chars emitted.

requirements.text: Document the bootstrap sequence: how M0-T023 is created, cites D-001, and proves the new regime on itself without circularly depending on enforcement code that does not exist yet; the final evidence distinguishes bootstrap actions performed under the old CLI from validations replayed under the completed new CLI.
requirements.required_evidence: BOOTSTRAP.md present; return separates old-CLI vs new-CLI-replayed actions.

requirements.text: The adversarial harness includes at minimum the amendment's listed set (missing/fake/withdrawn refs; omitted applicable requirements; multiple simultaneous directives; amendments and supersession; conflicts; corrupted manifests/hashes; legacy terminal immutability; every grandfathered nonterminal transition; stale post-review evidence; merge/rebase/squash; reminder size/dedup; hook failure; verifier read-only proof).
requirements.required_evidence: Each listed scenario has a named test.

requirements.text: Preserve the two-lane principle: the registry records authoritative owner instructions while all work-blocking decisions continue through the existing CLI, blockers, holds, and gates; shared read-only reference resolution is not a second gate or competing authority.
requirements.required_evidence: Acceptance authority stays in accept(); resolver only validates references; blocking flows through blockers/holds.
